design_philosophy.core_loop: Survive 20 real-time minutes by laying traps and hiding from burglars Harry and Marv
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Evasion under time pressure with permanent failure state (one life per run)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Kevin McCallister defending home through trap placement and stealth
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unforgiving single-life design combined with real-time survival timer creates high-stakes tension; game resets completely on capture rather than offering checkpoints or continues

difficulty.lives_system: One life only; capture by Harry or Marv results in immediate game over and full restart
difficulty.checkpointing: None; game resets entirely from beginning on capture
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player controls trap placement timing and hiding spot selection to manage threat avoidance; real-time 20-minute window creates external difficulty constraint

progression.upgrade_system: Trap arsenal available for deployment (specific trap types not detailed in source)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Access to hiding locations within house environment
replayability_hooks: Multiple trap placement strategies to experiment with
Variable burglar AI patterns to adapt to across playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Licensed IP adaptation providing familiar film setting
House layout serves as interactive environment telling evasion narrative

faction_indicators: Kevin McCallister vs Wet Bandits opposition
environmental_storytelling: House interior layout as defensive stronghold
Trap placement mechanics reinforcing home-defense fantasy
Hiding spots integrated into familiar domestic spaces

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Home Alone NES article)
